subject: ap-csp
theme: Programs are developed collaboratively through iterative and incremental processes, and errors are categorized by when and how they occur.
core_concepts: A computing innovation is any technology that uses computing and has changed how people live or work — from apps to embedded systems.
Iterative development involves repeated cycles of build-test-revise based on feedback; incremental development adds one feature at a time and tests after each addition.
Four error types: syntax (code won't run), runtime (crashes during execution), logic (runs but wrong output), overflow (value exceeds storage capacity).
Collaboration tools include pair programming (driver + navigator in real time), code review (post-writing peer examination), and version control (tracks changes, enables revert).
Program purpose is the goal it aims to achieve; program function is the specific behavior and outputs it produces given inputs — they can diverge.
Documentation (comments) improves code readability and maintainability for human readers without affecting program execution.
Testing can only verify behavior for tested inputs — no finite set of test cases guarantees a program is error-free for all possible inputs.
exam_tip: The CB frequently tests the distinction between iterative and incremental development, and the ability to classify error types from program behavior descriptions — know that a program that runs but gives wrong output has a logic error.
